Landing.heroDesc: I help diagnose the state of your body and calculate your biological age to set anti-age goals that work specifically for you. Without struggle, through awareness.

Philosophy.title: The Body is a System
Philosophy.desc1: "When a system lacks resources, it works on survival, not development. Anti-age is a body support system that gives it resources every day."
Philosophy.f1Desc: We study the current state and understand exactly what is missing right now.
Philosophy.f2Desc: We find the points of energy loss and restore balance through a care system.
Philosophy.f3Desc: We build a long-term life strategy that supports your health.

About.title: Valentina
About.desc1: My path began with medical education (general medicine) and work in eye microsurgery. This shaped my view of the body as a single, living system, not a set of individual symptoms.

DiagnosticPreview.title: Diagnostics is not a diagnosis
DiagnosticPreview.desc: This is a way to understand your body's needs and current resources in order to move forward consciously.
DiagnosticPreview.f1Desc: Determining the level of stress, deficiencies, and biological resources through questionnaires.
DiagnosticPreview.f2Desc: Forming guidelines and a personal recovery plan.

Services.title: How we build your system
Services.desc: Four steps to a new quality of life
Services.s1Desc: Initial request and discussion of your expectations from the program.
Services.s2Desc: Deep study of lifestyle, habits, and body condition.
Services.s3Desc: Choosing specific intervention points to achieve goals.
Services.s4Desc: Recommendations, procedures, and support on the way to the result.

Contact.title: Ready to listen to your body?
Contact.desc: If you feel that your body is missing something right now and you want to understand how to build a life so that it works to your advantage - I will be happy to help.
Contact.requestPlaceholder: Tell me briefly about your goals...

Pricing.desc: Choose the level of guidance that best suits your goals. Whether you need an essential overview or a comprehensive, ongoing partnership.
Pricing.essentialDesc: A foundational overview of your biological age and basic metrics.
Pricing.premiumDesc: Comprehensive analysis, ongoing support, and a personalized roadmap.
Pricing.faqDesc: Clear answers to help you make the best decision for your health.
Pricing.q1: Can I upgrade my plan later?
Pricing.q2: Are lab tests included in the price?
Pricing.q3: Is it covered by health insurance?

Diagnostics.desc: Objective tools to understand your body's current biological state. Use these assessments to track progress and identify areas for personalized intervention.
Diagnostics.t1Desc: Calculate your Total Daily Energy Expenditure (TDEE) based on your basal metabolic rate and activity level. Essential for nutritional planning and weight management.
Diagnostics.t2Desc: Determine if your body is aging faster or slower than your chronological years based on lifestyle biomarkers and recent laboratory results.
Diagnostics.p1Desc: 10-year risk assessment of fatal cardiovascular disease based on clinical metrics.
Diagnostics.p2Desc: Rapid cognitive assessment tool for baseline brain longevity and neuro-tracking.
Diagnostics.p3Desc: Evaluate your sleep-wake cycle alignment and optimization of cortisol timing.
Diagnostics.p4Desc: Diagnostic screening of the objective quality of your sleep.
Diagnostics.p5Desc: RUS-AUDIT standard test for assessing alcohol consumption habits.
Diagnostics.p6Desc: Fagerstrom test for clinical assessment of physical nicotine dependence.
Diagnostics.p7Desc: Screening for risk of sarcopenia (age-related loss of muscle mass and strength).
Diagnostics.p8Desc: Comprehensive assessment of 7 body systems across 14 indicators. Prime aging panel test.
Diagnostics.p9Desc: Clinical assessment of climacteric syndrome and menopausal symptom severity.
Diagnostics.deepDesc: Calculators provide the raw data, but Valentina provides the expert insight. Book a diagnostic review session to translate these numbers into a personalized, actionable anti-aging protocol.
Diagnostics.p10Desc: International Prostate Symptom Score for prostate symptoms assessment.
Diagnostics.p11Desc: International Index of Erectile Function for assessment.

EnergyCalculator.title: Interactive Energy Expenditure Calculator
EnergyCalculator.subtitle: Based on the laws of healthy nutrition and metabolism
EnergyCalculator.bmrDesc: Energy required to maintain life at rest.
EnergyCalculator.recTitle: Recommended caloric intake for your goal:
EnergyCalculator.lawDesc: The energy value of the diet must match the body's energy expenditure. Consuming calories above your daily norm (TDEE) leads to anabolic processes (weight gain), while a deficit leads to catabolic processes (weight loss).

ScoreCalculator.title: SCORE Calculator
ScoreCalculator.subtitle: Estimation of 10-year risk of fatal cardiovascular disease. Applicable for individuals 40–65 years old without established CVD.
ScoreCalculator.recLow: Maintaining a healthy lifestyle and re-evaluation in 5 years is recommended.
ScoreCalculator.recModerate: It is advisable to discuss lifestyle modifications (diet, activity) to prevent the risk from increasing.
ScoreCalculator.recHigh: High risk. Active modification of risk factors, BP and lipid control is required. Consult a doctor.
ScoreCalculator.recVeryHigh: Very high risk. Intensive therapy and strict control of all indicators under medical supervision is required.
ScoreCalculator.infoDesc: This calculation is indicative. The SCORE chart is not used in patients with already diagnosed CVD, type 1 and 2 diabetes mellitus, chronic kidney disease, or very high levels of individual risk factors.

CircadianCalculator.title: Circadian Rhythm Assessment
CircadianCalculator.subtitle: Determine your chronotype (MEQ-SA) to understand your individual daily rhythm patterns.
CircadianCalculator.introDesc: This test will help determine your chronotype—your individual daily rhythm characteristics. Please answer based on how you have felt in recent weeks.
CircadianCalculator.question: Question
CircadianCalculator.methodology: Based on the methodology of T. Horne and O. Ostberg (MEQ-SA)
CircadianCalculator.q1Text: Considering only your own “feeling best” rhythm, at what time would you get up if you were entirely free to plan your day?
CircadianCalculator.q2Text: Considering only your own “feeling best” rhythm, at what time would you go to bed if you were entirely free to plan your evening?
CircadianCalculator.q3Text: If there is a specific time at which you have to get up in the morning, to what extent do you depend on being woken up by an alarm clock?
CircadianCalculator.q4Text: Assuming adequate environmental conditions, how easy do you find getting up in the morning?
CircadianCalculator.q5Text: How alert do you feel during the first half hour after having woken in the morning?
CircadianCalculator.q6Text: How is your appetite during the first half-hour after having woken in the morning?
CircadianCalculator.q7Text: During the first half-hour after having woken in the morning, how tired do you feel?
CircadianCalculator.q8Text: When you have no commitments the next day, at what time do you go to bed compared to your usual bedtime?
CircadianCalculator.q9Text: You have decided to engage in some physical exercise. A friend suggests that you do this one hour twice a week and the best time for him is between 7:00 - 8:00 AM. Bearing in mind nothing else but your own "feeling best" rhythm, how do you think you would perform?
CircadianCalculator.q10Text: At what time in the evening do you feel tired and as a result in need of sleep?
CircadianCalculator.q11Text: You wish to be at your peak performance for a test which you know is going to be mentally exhausting and lasting for two hours. You are entirely free to plan your day and considering only your own "feeling best" rhythm which ONE of the four testing times would you choose?
CircadianCalculator.q12Text: If you went to bed at 11:00 PM at what level of tiredness would you be?
CircadianCalculator.q13Text: For some reason you have gone to bed several hours later than your usual bedtime, but there is no need to get up at any particular time the next morning. Which ONE of the following events are you most likely to experience?
CircadianCalculator.q13A: Will wake up at usual time and will NOT fall asleep
CircadianCalculator.q13B: Will wake up at usual time and will doze thereafter
CircadianCalculator.q13C: Will wake up at usual time but will fall asleep again
CircadianCalculator.q13D: Will NOT wake up until later than usual
CircadianCalculator.q14Text: One night you have to remain awake between 4:00 - 6:00 AM in order to carry out a night watch. You have no commitments the next day. Which ONE of the following alternatives will suit you best?
CircadianCalculator.q14A: Would NOT go to bed until watch was over
CircadianCalculator.q14B: Would take a nap before and sleep after
CircadianCalculator.q14C: Would take a good sleep before and nap after
CircadianCalculator.q14D: Would take ALL sleep before watch
CircadianCalculator.q15Text: You have to do two hours of hard physical work. You are entirely free to plan your day. Considering only your own "feeling best" rhythm which ONE of the following times would you choose?
CircadianCalculator.q16Text: You have decided to engage in hard physical exercise. A friend suggests that you do this one hour twice a week and the best time for him is between 10:00 - 11:00 PM. Bearing in mind nothing else but your own "feeling best" rhythm, how well do you think you would perform?
CircadianCalculator.q17Text: Suppose that you can choose your own work hours. Assume that you worked a FIVE hour day (including breaks) and that your job was interesting and paid by results. Which FIVE CONSECUTIVE HOURS would you select?
CircadianCalculator.q18Text: At what time of the day do you think that you reach your "feeling best" peak?
CircadianCalculator.q19Text: One hears about "morning" and "evening" types of people. Which ONE of these types do you consider yourself to be?
CircadianCalculator.q19B: Rather more a "morning" than an "evening" type
CircadianCalculator.q19C: Rather more an "evening" than a "morning" type
CircadianCalculator.res1Desc: You feel most energetic in the morning. Getting up early is easy, and your productivity peaks in the first half of the day. You tire quickly in the evening.
CircadianCalculator.res2Desc: You lean towards morning activity but have some flexibility. It's comfortable for you to wake up relatively early, but you can also work effectively during the day.
CircadianCalculator.res3Desc: You are an adaptive type. You lack distinct peaks of activity at extreme hours of the day. You adapt to social schedules without severe stress.
CircadianCalculator.res4Desc: Your activity is shifted to the afternoon. In the morning, you need time to get going, while your peak alertness occurs closer to the evening.
CircadianCalculator.res5Desc: You are a classic 'owl'. You are most productive late at night or even overnight. Waking up early is very difficult for you and may negatively affect your wellbeing.

InsomniaCalculator.title: Insomnia Severity Index (ISI)
InsomniaCalculator.subtitle: Please evaluate the nature of your sleep over the past 2 weeks.
InsomniaCalculator.res1Desc: Your indicators are within normal limits or slightly deviated.
InsomniaCalculator.res2Desc: Your indicators are within normal limits or slightly deviated.
InsomniaCalculator.res3Desc: A consultation with a specialist is recommended to clarify the diagnosis and select therapy.
InsomniaCalculator.res4Desc: A consultation with a specialist is recommended to clarify the diagnosis and select therapy.
InsomniaCalculator.methodology: Based on the clinical ISI questionnaire
InsomniaCalculator.disclaimer: This test is a screening tool and does not replace a full diagnosis by a somnologist. The results should be interpreted in the context of overall health.
InsomniaCalculator.q2Text: Severity of difficulty staying asleep (waking in the middle of the night):
InsomniaCalculator.q3Text: Severity of problem waking up too early:
InsomniaCalculator.q4Text: How SATISFIED/DISSATISFIED are you with your current sleep pattern?
InsomniaCalculator.q5Text: To what extent do you consider your sleep problem to INTERFERE with your daily functioning (e.g. daytime fatigue, ability to function at work/daily chores, concentration, memory, mood)?
InsomniaCalculator.q6Text: How NOTICEABLE to others do you think your sleep problem is in terms of impairing the quality of your life?
InsomniaCalculator.q7Text: How WORRIED/DISTRESSED are you about your current sleep problem?

MiniCogCalculator.title: Mini-Cog Test
MiniCogCalculator.subtitle: Cognitive impairment screening.
MiniCogCalculator.intro1: This test is used for rapid detection of dementia signs. It consists of a memory exercise and a clock drawing test.
MiniCogCalculator.intro2: Perform the test in a quiet environment. If testing another person, speak instructions clearly.
MiniCogCalculator.stage1Desc: Please remember the following three words. You will need to repeat them at the end of the test:
MiniCogCalculator.stage2Desc: Draw a clock face with all the numbers and hands so that they show
MiniCogCalculator.stage3Desc: Select the 3 words you memorized at the beginning:
MiniCogCalculator.evalClockDesc: Please answer honestly: are all the numbers in the right places and do the hands show exactly 11:10?
MiniCogCalculator.clockYes: Yes, the clock face and time are correct (2 points)
MiniCogCalculator.clockNo: No, there are mistakes / I couldn't draw it (0 points)
MiniCogCalculator.interpretationDesc: 2 points if the clock face is correct (numbers in place) and time is correct. 0 points if there are errors.
MiniCogCalculator.resBadDesc: A score below 3 indicates a high probability of cognitive impairment. Please consult a specialist (neurologist or geriatrician) for a more detailed examination.
MiniCogCalculator.resGoodDesc: A score of 3-5 indicates a low risk of pronounced cognitive impairment. However, this test is only a screening.

SystemicBioAge.title: Your Biological Age
SystemicBioAge.subtitle: Biological age shows the condition of your body and its systems. Take 7 simple tests to find out how many years your body feels.
SystemicBioAge.disclaimer: *This test is an assessment and does not replace a medical examination. Regular physical activity and a balanced diet are recommended to maintain youth.

cardio.title: Cardiovascular System
cardio.instr: 1. Measure resting pulse. 2. Do 60 squats in 1 minute. 3. Measure pulse again. Count the difference (how many beats it increased).

flexibility.title: Muscle Flexibility
flexibility.instr: Stand straight, feet together. Lean forward without bending knees. How deep could you bend?
options: Did not reach floor (up to 5 cm)

reaction.title: Reaction Speed
reaction.instr: Take 10 peas. Lift one hand high, hold the second low. Release peas from top and catch with bottom hand. How many caught?

coordination.title: Coordination
coordination.instr: Stand on tiptoes, feet shoulder-width apart, eyes closed, hands up. How many seconds did you maintain balance?

vestibular.title: Vestibular Apparatus
vestibular.instr: Make 60 rotations around your axis. Time how long it takes for dizziness to completely pass.

skin.title: Skin Elasticity
skin.instr: Pinch the skin on the back of your hand hard and hold for 5 seconds. In how many seconds does the pinch mark disappear?

joints.title: Condition of Joints
joints.instr: Bring one hand behind back from top, the other from bottom. Try to lock fingers. Repeat, changing hands.

BioAgeCalculator.title: Bio-Age Calculator
BioAgeCalculator.subtitle: Find out how young your body is compared to your calendar age based on body parameters.
BioAgeCalculator.infoDesc1: The calculation is based on the Body Mass Index (BMI) and Waist-to-Hip Ratio (WHR). Excess weight and especially fat accumulation around the waist (visceral fat) are associated with an increased risk of metabolic diseases, which mathematically "adds" years to you.
BioAgeCalculator.infoDesc2: Note: this calculator uses a generalized statistical formula and is not a medical diagnosis.

Client.subtitle: Your holistic wellness journey is blooming beautifully.
Client.maLimitError: File size too large. Maximum 15MB allowed.
Client.maTypeError: Unsupported file type. Please upload PDF or Images.
Client.upgDesc: Valentina is available for your monthly check-in. Together, we'll refine your path to timeless vitality.
Client.footer: Your health is a masterpiece in progress.

Results.title: My Results
Results.subtitle: Analysis and history of all your diagnostic screenings and tests.
Results.emptyDesc: You haven't completed any diagnostic tests yet. Visit the Diagnostics page to start.

Analysis.title: Stage 02: Analysis
Analysis.description: Deep study of lifestyle, habits, and body condition based on TG bot data and questionnaires.
Analysis.noData: Insufficient data for full analysis. Please complete questionnaires and maintain your TG bot diary.
Analysis.stagedSubtitle: Review the data that will be sent to the AI for analysis. You can go back to complete missing info if needed.

AlcoholCalculator.title: RUS-AUDIT Assessment
AlcoholCalculator.subtitle: Alcohol Consumption Risk Assessment (WHO Golden Standard)
AlcoholCalculator.intro1: This assessment helps evaluate your alcohol consumption patterns and identify potential associated health risks.
AlcoholCalculator.intro2: Please answer the following 10 questions honestly based on your experience over the past year.
AlcoholCalculator.question: Question
AlcoholCalculator.methodology: Based on the World Health Organization (WHO) AUDIT methodology.
AlcoholCalculator.disclaimer: This test is not a definitive diagnosis. It serves as a preliminary screening tool.
AlcoholCalculator.q1Text: How often do you have a drink containing alcohol?
AlcoholCalculator.q2Text: How many drinks containing alcohol do you have on a typical day when you are drinking?
AlcoholCalculator.q2Info: 1 portion = 10 g of pure alcohol (approx. 30 ml of vodka, 100 ml of wine, or 250 ml of beer)
AlcoholCalculator.q3Text: How often do you have 6 or more drinks on one occasion?
AlcoholCalculator.q4Text: How often during the last year have you found that you were not able to stop drinking once you had started?
AlcoholCalculator.q5Text: How often during the last year have you failed to do what was normally expected of you because of drinking?
AlcoholCalculator.q6Text: How often during the last year have you needed a first drink in the morning to get yourself going after a heavy drinking session?
AlcoholCalculator.q7Text: How often during the last year have you had a feeling of guilt or remorse after drinking?
AlcoholCalculator.q8Text: How often during the last year have you been unable to remember what happened the night before because of your drinking?
AlcoholCalculator.q9Text: Have you or someone else been injured because of your drinking?
AlcoholCalculator.q9O2: Yes, but not in the last year
AlcoholCalculator.q10Text: Has a relative, friend, doctor, or other health care worker been concerned about your drinking or suggested you cut down?
AlcoholCalculator.q10O2: Yes, but not in the last year
AlcoholCalculator.res1FullDesc: Your alcohol consumption level is considered safe for the health of most people.
AlcoholCalculator.res1Rec: Continue to practice moderation. Remember to have 'alcohol-free days' during the week.
AlcoholCalculator.res2Desc: Risk of medical and social consequences.
AlcoholCalculator.res2FullDesc: Your alcohol consumption level increases the risk of developing health and social problems.
AlcoholCalculator.res2Rec: It is recommended to reduce the amount and frequency of alcohol consumption. Familiarize yourself with the principles of a healthy lifestyle.
AlcoholCalculator.res3FullDesc: There is a high risk of dependency formation and serious organ damage (liver, heart, brain).
AlcoholCalculator.res3Rec: Specialist consultation and brief motivational counseling are required. Consider completely abstaining from alcohol.
AlcoholCalculator.res4FullDesc: The indicators point to a high probability of alcohol dependence.
AlcoholCalculator.res4Rec: It is strongly recommended to consult a specialized professional (narcologist or psychotherapist) to assess your condition and develop a treatment plan.

NicotineCalculator.title: Fagerstrom Test
NicotineCalculator.subtitle: Clinical standard for assessing nicotine dependence
NicotineCalculator.introDesc: This test will help determine how physically dependent your body is on nicotine. This is critically important for choosing the right smoking cessation method.
NicotineCalculator.q1Text: How soon after you wake up do you smoke your first cigarette?
NicotineCalculator.q2Text: Do you find it difficult to refrain from smoking in places where it is forbidden?
NicotineCalculator.q3Text: Which cigarette would you hate most to give up?
NicotineCalculator.q3O1: The first one in the morning
NicotineCalculator.q4Text: How many cigarettes per day do you smoke?
NicotineCalculator.q5Text: Do you smoke more frequently during the first hours after waking than during the rest of the day?
NicotineCalculator.q6Text: Do you smoke if you are so ill that you are in bed most of the day?
NicotineCalculator.resDesc1: You have mainly a psychological habit. Physical craving for nicotine is minimal.
NicotineCalculator.resAdvice1: It will be easiest for you to quit 'cold turkey'. The main task is to replace rituals (coffee with a cigarette, smoking breaks with colleagues) with healthy habits.
NicotineCalculator.resDesc2: Physical dependence has begun to form, but your body does not yet critically demand nicotine.
NicotineCalculator.resAdvice2: Use the 'abrupt quitting' method. Start taking vitamins and increase physical activity to ease mild discomfort in the first 3 days.
NicotineCalculator.resDesc3: Moderate dependence. You often use smoking to regulate your emotional state.
NicotineCalculator.resAdvice3: Consider mild forms of nicotine replacement therapy (gum, patch). It's important to plan in advance how to cope with stress without cigarettes.
NicotineCalculator.resDesc4: Strong physical craving. When trying to quit, you will likely encounter severe withdrawal symptoms.
NicotineCalculator.resAdvice4: Medical consultation is recommended to prescribe medications (varenicline, cytisine, or NRT). Combine medication with breathing practices.
NicotineCalculator.resDesc5: Your body is strictly adapted to high doses of nicotine. Quitting on your own is extremely difficult.
NicotineCalculator.resAdvice5: Do not try to cope alone—this can lead to relapse and depression. Contact a Health Center or an addiction specialist for a treatment plan.
NicotineCalculator.actionNote: Make the decision to quit today—it's the best investment in your future.
NicotineCalculator.motivationIntro: Why is this important right now?
NicotineCalculator.motivationText: When you quit smoking, the risk of excess cardiovascular mortality drops by half within 10 years, and after 30 years it equals that of non-smokers.

ProfileSettings.title: Profile Settings
ProfileSettings.subtitle: Manage your personal information and profile picture
ProfileSettings.error: Failed to update profile. Please try again.

Auth.title: Platform Access
Auth.subtitle: Sign in or create an account to continue using personalized Longevity Portal protocols.
Auth.errorRegisterFailed: Registration failed. User may already exist.
Auth.successRegister: Account created! Please check your email and you can now sign in.

SarcfCalculator.title: SARC-F Questionnaire
SarcfCalculator.subtitle: Screening for risk of sarcopenia and age-related changes in the muscular system.
SarcfCalculator.intro1: SARC-F is a simple and quick screening tool used to identify the risk of sarcopenia (age-related loss of muscle mass, strength, and function).
SarcfCalculator.question: Question
SarcfCalculator.methodology: Based on the SARC-F clinical questionnaire.
SarcfCalculator.disclaimer: This test is a primary screening tool and does not replace medical diagnosis.
SarcfCalculator.q1Text: How much difficulty do you have in lifting and carrying as much as 10 pounds (4.5 kg)?
SarcfCalculator.q2Text: How much difficulty do you have walking across a room?
SarcfCalculator.q2O2: A lot, use aids, or unable
SarcfCalculator.q3Text: How much difficulty do you have rising from a chair or bed?
SarcfCalculator.q3O2: A lot or unable without help
SarcfCalculator.q4Text: How much difficulty do you have climbing a flight of 10 stairs?
SarcfCalculator.q5Text: How many times have you fallen in the past year?
SarcfCalculator.resNormalDesc: No symptoms of sarcopenia detected. Your muscle status is normal.
SarcfCalculator.resNormalRec: Continue to follow the principles of healthy nutrition and maintain regular physical activity to preserve muscle health.
SarcfCalculator.resHighDesc: Your result indicates a high probability of sarcopenia presence.
SarcfCalculator.resHighRec: It is recommended to consult a doctor for additional tests (measurement of muscle mass and strength) and for the appointment of preventive or therapeutic measures.

DiagnosticModal.regionPlaceholder: e.g. London, risk of Vit D deficiency
DiagnosticModal.heredityPlaceholder: Diabetes in father, early heart attack in mom...
DiagnosticModal.loadingDesc: We are matching your symptoms with age norms and longevity biomarkers.
DiagnosticModal.errorMessage: An error occurred while generating the report. Please try again later.
DiagnosticModal.readyDesc: Based on your data, we will generate a personalized list of tests to assess biological age.
DiagnosticModal.regDesc: Enter your details to receive your personalized test list via email
DiagnosticModal.aiOfferDesc: Personal health monitoring, test interpretation, and daily recommendations in your Telegram.
DiagnosticModal.premiumOfferDesc: Full specialist support, development of a personalized rejuvenation protocol, and priority access.
DiagnosticModal.warningDesc: This list is a preliminary AI recommendation. The final decision should be made by a specialist during an in-person appointment.

GreeneScale.title: Greene Scale
GreeneScale.subtitle: Assessment of climacteric syndrome severity
GreeneScale.aboutDesc: The Greene Climacteric Scale helps assess and quantify menopausal symptoms. Please indicate the extent to which you have being bothered by the following symptoms during the past week.
GreeneScale.resultsDesc: Scores by domains of the Greene Scale
GreeneScale.disclaimer: Please note: This questionnaire is a screening tool and does not replace consultation with a physician.

questions.q1: Do you feel your heart beating quickly or strongly?
questions.q2: Do you feel tense or nervous?
questions.q3: Do you have difficulty sleeping? (Either getting to sleep or waking up in the night?)
questions.q5: Are you anxious or experiencing panic attacks?
questions.q7: Are you tired or lacking in energy during the day?
questions.q8: Have you experienced a lack of interest in things and activities?
questions.q9: Do you often feel unhappy or depressed?
questions.q10: Are you experiencing crying spells in your day-to-day life?
questions.q11: Are you irritable with your family, friends, or contacts?
questions.q12: Do you get dizzy or feel like you are close to fainting?
questions.q13: Do you feel pressure or a tightness in your head/body?
questions.q14: Do parts of your body feel numb or tingling?
questions.q16: Do you feel pains or aches in your muscles and/or joints?
questions.q17: Do you ever experience a loss of feeling or numbness in your hands and feet?
questions.q20: Do you sweat during the night (night sweats)?
questions.q21: Has your libido changed? Have you lost interest in sex?

low.label: Mild symptoms
low.desc: Your scores indicate a mild degree of climacteric syndrome or its absence. Symptoms do not significantly affect the quality of life.

medium.label: Moderate symptoms
medium.desc: Climacteric syndrome of moderate severity. Symptoms may cause discomfort. A scheduled consultation with a specialist is recommended to discuss relief methods.

high.label: Severe symptoms
high.desc: Severe climacteric syndrome, significantly affecting the quality of life and well-being. It is strongly recommended to consult a doctor to select corrective therapy.

IPSS.title: IPSS Questionnaire
IPSS.subtitle: International Prostate Symptom Score
IPSS.aboutDesc: Please answer the questions by choosing the one option that best describes your condition.
IPSS.disclaimer: This questionnaire is a screening tool. For an accurate diagnosis, be sure to consult a urologist.

questions.q1: 1. Over the past month, how often have you had a sensation of not emptying your bladder completely after you finished urinating?
questions.q2: 2. Over the past month, how often have you had to urinate again less than two hours after you finished urinating?
questions.q3: 3. Over the past month, how often have you found you stopped and started again several times when you urinated?
questions.q4: 4. Over the past month, how often have you found it difficult to postpone urination?
questions.q5: 5. Over the past month, how often have you had a weak urinary stream?
questions.q6: 6. Over the past month, how often have you had to push or strain to begin urination?
questions.q7: 7. Over the past month, how many times did you most typically get up to urinate from the time you went to bed at night until the time you got up in the morning?
questions.q8: 8. If you were to spend the rest of your life with your urinary condition just the way it is now, how would you feel about that?

low.label: Mild symptoms
low.desc: The patient is shown further observation, symptomatic therapy may be prescribed.

medium.label: Moderate symptoms
medium.desc: The patient is shown further examination to select drugs for conservative treatment.

high.label: Severe symptoms
high.desc: The patient is shown active treatment, further examination to decide on surgical or complex drug intervention.

MIEF5.title: IIEF-5 Questionnaire
MIEF5.subtitle: International Index of Erectile Function
MIEF5.aboutDesc: The International Index of Erectile Function (IIEF-5) is a scale for assessing erectile dysfunction severity. Please answer the questions as honestly and clearly as possible (regarding the last four weeks).
MIEF5.disclaimer: This questionnaire is a screening tool. For an accurate diagnosis, be sure to consult a urologist or andrologist.

questions.q1: How often have you had an erection during sexual activity recently?
questions.q2: Over the past month, how often has the erection been sufficient for penetration (beginning intercourse)?
questions.q3: When attempting sexual intercourse, how often have you been able to achieve penetration?
questions.q4: Over the past month, how often have you been able to maintain an erection after starting intercourse?
questions.q5: How difficult has it been to maintain an erection throughout and until the end of intercourse?

light.label: Mild erectile dysfunction
light.desc: Consultation with a specialist is recommended for prevention.

medium.label: Moderate erectile dysfunction
medium.desc: It is recommended to consult a urologist-andrologist to select therapy.

heavy.label: Severe erectile dysfunction
heavy.desc: It is strongly recommended to consult a doctor for a detailed examination.

Water.prompt:
💧 **Water Registration**

How many milliliters (ml) did you drink?
